headline: US midterms countdown: the GOP courts Hispanic voters
summary: The US economy is expected to have rebounded in Q3 after contracting in the first half of 2022
body_text:
This is an audio transcript of the FT News Briefing podcast episode: ‘US midterms countdown — the GOP courts Hispanic voters’

Marc FilippinoGood morning from the Financial Times. Today is Thursday, October 27th. And this is your FT News Briefing.

[MUSIC PLAYING]

A global energy expert sees a peak in fossil fuel demand by the end of the decade, and the US economy is still going strong. Plus, in our US midterm election series, we’re gonna go to Pennsylvania to look at how the Republican party is making a grab for the fastest-growing voter demographic.

Ronna McDanielGracias por la invitación. Es un placer estar aquí. (Crowd cheering)

Marc FilippinoI’m Marc Filippino. And here’s the news you need to start your day.

[MUSIC PLAYING]

The head of the International Energy Agency says Russia’s invasion of Ukraine will accelerate a shift away from fossil fuels. Fatih Birol told the Financial Times he expects fossil fuels, including gas, to reach peak demand by the end of the decade. Birol
